description: Routine should produce a weighted comparison of 3+ candidates with explicit characteristics, scores, rationale per score, recommendation, and what-would-change-my-mind section.

input_ticket.title: Evaluate job queue options for SIA: Postgres-backed (pg-boss) vs Redis (BullMQ) vs managed (Cloudflare Queues)
input_ticket.description: SIA needs a job queue for async ingestion + publishing. Three candidates: pg-boss (reuses existing Postgres), BullMQ (Redis-backed, proven), Cloudflare Queues (managed). Score against characteristics the app actually needs.

smoke_questions: Are the weights in the decision criteria genuinely differentiated (not all 15–20%)?
Does each score (1–5) have a one-sentence rationale, not just a number?
Does the recommendation cite the specific criterion(s) that tilted it?
Is there a criterion whose change would flip the recommendation, named explicitly?
Is the implementation plan bounded (time, milestones) rather than 'we'll figure it out'?
success_criteria: 3+ candidates + weights sum to 100 + every cell has rationale + what-would-change-my-mind non-empty
